aliases: How to configure Jest for TypeScript projects
Jest 30 configuration with ts-jest and ESM
Jest setup for React and Node.js testing

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Jest 30 (June 2025) — dropped Node <18, jsdom upgraded to 26, stricter TypeScript types

constraints: Jest 30 requires Node.js 18+ — versions 14, 16, 19, and 21 are no longer supported
TypeScript config files (jest.config.ts) require ts-node or esbuild-register installed
ESM support requires --experimental-vm-modules flag — not yet stable without it
jest-environment-jsdom is a separate package since Jest 28 — must be installed explicitly
transform configuration must match your file types — .ts/.tsx files need ts-jest or @swc/jest

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Using Vite and want a Vite-native test runner
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Vitest — drop-in Jest replacement for Vite projects

inputs_needed.question: Are you testing TypeScript or JavaScript?
Are you testing browser code or Node.js?
Does your project use ESM or CommonJS?
